item.title: Hezbollah accepts U.S. proposal for mutual halt to attacks with Israel, Lebanese embassy says
item.description:
BEIRUT, June 1 (Reuters) - Lebanon's embassy in Washington said in a statement on Monday that Hezbollah had accepted a U.S. proposal for a mutual cessation of hostilities that would be extended to encompass all Lebanese territory.

The statement, shared by the Lebanese presidency on X, said the arrangement would first see Israel refrain from striking Beirut's southern suburbs in exchange for Iran-backed Hezbollah halting its attacks against Israel, after Israel had threatened to attack the capital's suburbs on Monday.

item.title: 'We are in a war,' alleged Iran-backed militant exclaims in US court
item.description:
By Luc Cohen

NEW YORK, June 1 (Reuters) - An alleged member of an Iran-backed militia pleaded not guilty on Monday to U.S. criminal charges he was involved in multiple attacks against American interests in Europe, proclaiming in a courtroom outburst, "I am not a criminal."

item.title: Trump says Israel, Hezbollah agree to halt fighting
item.description:
US President Donald Trump said Monday that Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to stop fighting, adding that Iran talks were moving rapidly after seeming to be on the rocks over Israel's Lebanon offensive.

Trump said on social media that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had promised not to send troops to Beirut's southern suburbs as threatened, while Hezbollah had agreed that "all shooting will stop."

item.title: Trump says Hezbollah, Israel agree to stop fighting after call with Netanyahu
item.description: Neither Hezbollah nor Israel has confirmed the ceasefire announced by President Donald Trump, who made the declaration after Iran's IRGC threatened a major attack on northern Israel and warned it could close the Bab al-Mandeb strait if Israel followed through on plans to strike Beirut.

item.title: Austria puts Syrian ex-intelligence chief in Raqqa on trial for torture
item.description:
By Francois Murphy

VIENNA, June 1 (Reuters) - A former Syrian intelligence chief in the city of Raqqa went on trial in Austria on charges of torture and sexual abuse on Monday over the mistreatment of opponents of then-leader Bashar al-Assad more than a decade ago.

It is a relatively rare case of a European country claiming jurisdiction for alleged crimes committed by agents of Assad's government. The trial, due to last a month, will include alleged victims' testimony.

item.title: Trump says no Israeli troops will go to Beirut after call with Netanyahu
item.description:
June 1 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that Israel would send no troops to Beirut after a call he held with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

"I had a very productive call with Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu, of Israel, and there will be no Troops going to Beirut, and any Troops that are on their way, have already been turned back,” Trump said in a post on Truth Social.

"Likewise, through highly placed Representatives, I had a very good call with Hezbollah, and they agreed that all shooting will stop."

item.title: Still too risky to move seafarers out of Gulf, U.N. agency boss says
item.description:
By Jonathan Saul and Renee Maltezou

ATHENS, June 1 (Reuters) - Despite the current ceasefire between the United States and Iran, it remains too risky to move the thousands of sailors stuck in the Gulf, the head of the U.N.'s shipping agency said.

item.title: Trump says he has not heard from Iran that they are suspending talks, NBC News reports
item.description:
June 1 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that he had not heard from Iranians that they were suspending talks with the Washington, but added that silence would be fine and he was willing to wait.

"I think we've been talking too much if you want to know the truth. I think going silent would be very good, and that could be for a long time," Trump said in an interview with NBC News.

"It doesn't mean we're going to go and start dropping bombs all over there," Trump was quoted as saying. "We'll just go silent. We'll keep the blockade."

item.title: Iran eyes limited US deal to relieve economic strain and buy time
item.description:
By Parisa Hafezi

DUBAI, June 1 (Reuters) - Iran is pushing for a limited interim agreement with the United States in a bid to ease mounting economic pressure and stabilise the situation at home, while avoiding major concessions on its nuclear programme, according to sources and analysts.

The approach reflects a familiar playbook for the Islamic Republic: absorb pressure, avoid irreversible compromises and keep negotiations alive without shifting core positions, three Iranian sources close to decision-makers said.

item.title: Oil prices jump as Iran suspends peace talks
item.description:
Oil prices jumped and equities struggled on Monday as Middle East peace talks stumbled and tensions mounted between Iran and the United States.

Crude futures shot around seven percent higher after an Iranian news agency announced Tehran had suspended the negotiations with Washington via mediators.

The United States and Iran had traded strikes over the weekend and Tehran had insisted that any deal to end the war must cover Israel's escalating offensive into Lebanon.

item.title: Turkey maritime law prompts Greek rethink on Aegean marine parks
item.description: The debate risks adding a new irritant to a fragile Turkey-Greece thaw that followed years of tensions.

item.title: Turkey maritime law prompts Greek rethink on Aegean marine parks
item.description: The debate risks adding a new irritant to a fragile Turkey-Greece thaw that followed years of tensions.

item.title: Turkey maritime law prompts Greek rethink on Aegean marine parks
item.description: The debate risks adding a new irritant to a fragile Turkey-Greece thaw that followed years of tensions.

item.title: Turkey maritime law prompts Greek rethink on Aegean marine parks
item.description: The debate risks adding a new irritant to a fragile Turkey-Greece thaw that followed years of tensions.

item.title: Lebanon crusader castle seized by Israel a symbol of bloody history
item.description:
By Angus McDowall and Benjamin Raab

JERUSALEM/BEIRUT, June 1 (Reuters) - The medieval Beaufort Castle's stone ramparts tower so far above south Lebanon that Israel could hail their capture as a strategic victory, even as it evoked the miseries of a previous occupation.

When Israeli forces entered the 900-year-old castle on Saturday, they were returning to a fortress they held throughout their 1982-2000 presence in southern Lebanon, a campaign that years of Hezbollah attacks ultimately drove them to end.

item.title: Iraq arrests deputy oil minister in anti-corruption push
item.description: The arrest of Adnan al-Jumaili marks a second signification step against corruption by Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi’s government amid growing pressure from the Trump administration.

item.title: Iraq arrests deputy oil minister in anti-corruption push
item.description: The arrest of Adnan al-Jumaili marks a second signification step against corruption by Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi’s government amid growing pressure from the Trump administration.

item.title: Iraq arrests deputy oil minister in anti-corruption push
item.description: The arrest of Adnan al-Jumaili marks a second signification step against corruption by Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi’s government amid growing pressure from the Trump administration.

item.description: The arrest of Adnan al-Jumaili marks a second signification step against corruption by Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi’s government amid growing pressure from the Trump administration.

item.title: Iran suspends US talks over Israel’s Lebanon escalation, state media reports: What to know
item.description: Iran’s Tasnim News Agency also said Tehran could "activate other fronts," including closure of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.
